config.rationale: The next run should stay on vLLM because it is the clear leader on throughput and latency, and the last vLLM tuning pass (chunked prefill + prefix caching + higher memory util + language-model-only) did not beat the simpler baseline. For optimize_throughput, the highest-value untested lever now is FP8 on top of the winning vLLM baseline, while keeping the rest of the configuration close to the current best point so the delta is attributable. I kept TP=1/PP=1/DP=1 because there is only one GPU, max_model_len=65536 because that context size is already proven safe and larger KV reservation would likely reduce throughput on a single 80GB H100, gpu_memory_utilization=0.9 because the 0.95 variant did not help, and baseline defaults elsewhere per the rules. Expected outcome is roughly +8% to +20% throughput versus the current best vLLM baseline if FP8 is stable on this model/H100, with little TTFT benefit and some risk of flat or slightly worse latency. For the user’s exact SGLang MTP command at context-length 262144 (NEXTN, 3 steps, topk 1, 4 draft tokens), my estimate is only a modest net gain, roughly +5% to +15% throughput versus non-speculative SGLang at the same 262144 context if it becomes healthy; confidence is limited because a smaller-context speculative SGLang attempt already failed health checks, and the very large KV reservation at 262144 will eat batching headroom and reduce the realized speculative win. For vLLM, the isolated gain from --language-model-only on this VLM checkpoint is likely modest: about +0% to +8% throughput at fixed context/concurrency, mostly from reclaiming VRAM by skipping vision components; the upside is higher only if that saved memory converts into materially higher admitted concurrency or KV cache. Since the combined language-model-only run already failed to improve over the plain vLLM baseline, FP8 is the stronger next throughput bet.

smoke_tests.json_mode_detail: No content in response (reasoning model may not support JSON mode)

llm_commentary: Experiment `be1182b06ae3` is the new throughput leader at 6355.3, a roughly 10.9% gain over the prior best 5728.7, so it clearly advances the throughput objective. It is not the latency leader because TTFT p95 rose to 32.7 ms versus the 26.9 ms best, but it remains very low in absolute terms and still sits on the Pareto front. For the balanced objective, it is now the strongest result as well, delivering the highest throughput among runs comfortably under the 500 ms TTFT threshold.
docker_command: docker run --name bench-vllm-be1182b06ae3 --gpus all --shm-size 16g --network host -v /root/.cache/huggingface:/root/.cache/huggingface -d vllm/vllm-openai:latest --model Qwen/Qwen3.5-9B --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --tensor-parallel-size 1 --dtype auto --gpu-memory-utilization 0.9 --max-model-len 65536 --quantization fp8 --enable-auto-tool-choice --tool-call-parser qwen3_coder --reasoning-parser qwen3
